questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body starting from rest moves with uniform acceleration and reaches 20 m/s in 5 s. Its acceleration is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The SI unit of electric field is:
questions.explanation: Electric field E = F/q, so unit is newton per coulomb (N/C), same as V/m.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A force of 10 N acts on a 2 kg mass. The acceleration produced is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The kinetic energy of a 2 kg body moving at 3 m/s is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Which of the following is a vector quantity?
questions.explanation: Velocity has both magnitude and direction, so it is a vector.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The time period of a simple pendulum (small amplitude) depends on its:
questions.explanation: T = 2π√(L/g); it depends on length L (and g), not on mass.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Using Ohm's law, if V = 10 V and R = 5 Ω, the current I is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The work done by a force acting perpendicular to displacement is:
questions.explanation: W = F·d·cosθ; at θ = 90°, cos90° = 0, so work done is zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The approximate acceleration due to gravity on Earth's surface is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The SI unit of linear momentum is:
questions.explanation: Momentum p = mv, so unit is kg·m/s (equal to N·s).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A car accelerates from rest at 2 m/s² for 10 s. Find its final velocity (in m/s).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A 5 kg object is lifted to a height of 4 m. Taking g = 10 m/s², the work done against gravity (in joules) is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two 6 Ω resistors are connected in parallel. The equivalent resistance (in ohms) is:

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: The atomic number of carbon is:
questions.explanation: Carbon has 6 protons, so its atomic number is 6.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: The pH of a neutral aqueous solution at 25°C is:
questions.explanation: A neutral solution at 25°C has pH = 7.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: The most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere is:
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the atmosphere.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: The chemical formula of common salt is:
questions.explanation: Common table salt is sodium chloride, NaCl.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: The number of electrons in a neutral oxygen atom is:
questions.explanation: Oxygen's atomic number is 8, so a neutral atom has 8 electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Which of the following is a noble gas?
questions.explanation: Neon belongs to Group 18, the noble gases.

questions.text: An acid turns blue litmus paper:

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Which of the following is a covalent compound?
questions.explanation: Water (H₂O) is formed by sharing of electrons — a covalent compound.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: The most electronegative element is:
questions.explanation: Fluorine is the most electronegative element.
